duas.title: Salat Jafar e Tayyar
duas.description:
Salat Jafar Tayyar For Special needs
Imam ( a.s.) told me: O Mufaddal! When you have a need, you must recite Namaz-e-Ja’far-e-Tayyar and then recite this Dua and beseech the Almighty for your needs so that it is fulfilled.
Friday is the best day for Namaz-e-Ja’far-e-Tayyar.
METHOD - Four rakat Salat, divided into two parts 2 x 2 rakat. Recite each part like the Fajr salat, with the following adjustments:
First rakat After recitation of Sura Faatihah, recite Sura Zilzaal (no.99) once Second rakat After recitation of Sura Faatihah, recite Sura Aadiyaat (no.100) once Third Rakat After recitation of Sura Faatihah, recite Sura Nasr (no.110) once Fourth Rakat after recitation of Sura Faatihah, recite Sura Ikhlaas(no.112) once.
if you do not remember these surahs of each rakat, then recite sura Ikhlaas in all 4 rakats
In each rakat recite Tasbeehaatul Arbaa 75 times as explained below
ARABIC TEXT
سُبْحَانَ اللهِ وَ الْحَمْدُ لِلهِ وَ لَا اِلٰهَ اِلَّا اللهُ وَ اللهُ اَكْبَر
subhaanallaahi wal hamdu lillaahi wa laa ilaaha illallaahu wallaahu akbar
Glory be to Allah, Praise be to Allah, there is no god but Allah, and Allah is Great.
(i) After reciation of Surahs… 15 times
(ii) In Rukoo… 10 times
(iii) In Qiyyaam after Rukoo… 10 times
(iv) In the First Sajdah… 10 times
(v) While sitting after First Sajdah… 10 times
(vi) In the second Sajdah… 10 times
(vii) While sitting after second Sajdah … 10 times. ( including in second rakat 'before' reciting salam)
he has to make an effort to remember to recite this at each movement since the automatic action is to go to next action in normal salaat. In case the recitation is missed in any position , those tasbihaat may be recited at the end upon completion
In case of old age/weakness ,one can recite Tasbeehaatul arbaa 300 times at the end of Namaz, according to your convenience, while sitting, lying or standing.
In the last sajda of 4th Rakat say following dua..
duas.arabic:
سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لَبِسَ ٱلْعِزَّ وَٱلْوَقَارَ
سُبْحَانَ مَنْ تَعَطَّفَ بِٱلْمَجْدِ وَتَكَرَّمَ بِهِ
سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لاَ يَنْبَغِي ٱلتَّسْبِيحُ إِلاَّ لَهُ
سُبْحَانَ مَنْ احْصَىٰ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمُهُ
سُبْحَانَ ذِي ٱلْمَنِّ وَٱلنِّعَمِ
سُبْحَانَ ذِي ٱلْقُدْرَةِ وَٱلْكَرَمِ
اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي اسْالُكَ بِمَعَاقِدِ ٱلْعِزِّ مِنْ عَرْشِكَ
وَمُنْتَهَىٰ ٱلرَّحْمَةِ مِنْ كِتَابِكَ
وَٱسْمِكَ ٱلاعْظَمِ
وَكَلِمَاتِكَ ٱلتَّامَّةِ
ٱلَّتِي تَمَّتْ صِدْقاً وَعَدْلاًَ
صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَاهْلِ بَيْتِهِ…
duas.transliteration:
subhana man labisa al`izza walwaqara
subhana man ta`attafa bilmajdi wa takarrama bihi
subhana man la yanbaghi alttasbihu illa lahu
subhana man ahsa kulla shay'in `ilmuhu
subhana dhi almanni walnni`ami
subhana dhi alqudrati walkarami
allahumma inni as'aluka bima`aqidi al`izzi min `arshika
wa muntaha alrrahmati min kitabika
wasmika al-a`zami
wa kalimatika alttammati
allati tammat sidqan wa `adlan
salli `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi…
duas.translation:
Glory be to Him Who has dressed Himself dignity and sobriety!
Glory be to Him Who has deigned and condescended with grandeur!
Glory be to Him save Whom none is worthy of being glorified!
Glory be to Him Whose knowledge has counted all things!
Glory be to the Lord of bounty and boon!
Glory be to the Lord of omnipotence and nobility!
O Allah, I beseech You in the name of the objects of dignity in Your Throne,
in the name of the utmost of mercy in Your Book,
and in the name of Your Greatest Name
and Your Accomplished Words,
which have been accomplished truly and justly,
(please) send blessings to Muhammad and his Household…

segments.translation: Glory be to Him Who has dressed Himself dignity and sobriety!

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ تَعَطَّفَ بِٱلْمَجْدِ وَتَكَرَّمَ بِهِ
segments.transliteration: subhana man ta`attafa bilmajdi wa takarrama bihi
segments.translation: Glory be to Him Who has deigned and condescended with grandeur!

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لاَ يَنْبَغِي ٱلتَّسْبِيحُ إِلاَّ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: subhana man la yanbaghi alttasbihu illa lahu
segments.translation: Glory be to Him save Whom none is worthy of being glorified!

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ احْصَىٰ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمُهُ
segments.translation: Glory be to Him Whose knowledge has counted all things!
Glory be to the Lord of bounty and boon!
Glory be to the Lord of omnipotence and nobility!

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي اسْالُكَ بِمَعَاقِدِ ٱلْعِزِّ مِنْ عَرْشِكَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka bima`aqidi al`izzi min `arshika
segments.translation: O Allah, I beseech You in the name of the objects of dignity in Your Throne,
in the name of the utmost of mercy in Your Book,
and in the name of Your Greatest Name
and Your Accomplished Words,
which have been accomplished truly and justly,
(please) send blessings to Muhammad and his Household…

duas.title: Dua after Salat Jafar e Tayyar
duas.description:
After the Salaat, Recite the following
Say each of these as many times as possible in one breath.
duas.arabic:
يَا رَبِّ يَا رَبِّ

يَا رَبَّاهُ يَا رَبَّاهُ

رَبِّ رَبِّ

يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ

يَا حَيُّ يَا حَيُّ

يَا رَحِيمُ يَا رَحِيمُ
THEN Repeat seven times.
يَا رَحْمٰنُ يَا رَحْمٰنُ
THEN Repeat seven times.
يَا ارْحَمَ ٱلرَّاحِمِينَ
duas.transliteration:
ya rabbi ya rabbi

ya rabbahu ya rabbahu

rabbi rabbi

ya allahu ya allahu

ya hayyu ya hayyu

ya rahimu ya rahimu

.
ya rahmanu ya rahmanu

.
ya arhama alrrahimina
duas.translation:
O Lord, O Lord!

O my Lord, O my Lord!

Lord, Lord!

O Allah, O Allah!

O Ever-living, O Ever-living!

O All-merciful, O All-merciful!

.
O All-compassionate, O All-compassionate!

.
O Most Merciful of all those who show mercy!

segments.translation: O Lord, O Lord!
O my Lord, O my Lord!
Lord, Lord!
O Allah, O Allah!
O Ever-living, O Ever-living!
O All-merciful, O All-merciful!
O All-compassionate, O All-compassionate!
O Most Merciful of all those who show mercy!

duas.title: Another Dua After Salat Jafar e Tayyar
duas.description: Al-Mufaddal then added: Imam Ja`far al-Sadiq (`a) then said to me, “If you have a significant request and you want it to be granted, you may offer this prayer, say this supplication, and implore Almighty Allah to respond to you. If you do, it will be granted, Allah willing.”
duas.arabic:
اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي افْتَتِحُ ٱلْقَوْلَ بِحَمْدِكَ

وَانْطِقُ بِٱلثَّنَاءِ عَلَيْكَ

وَامَجِّدُكَ وَلاَ غَايَةَ لِمَدْحِكَ

وَاثْنِي عَلَيْكَ وَمَنْ يَبْلُغُ غَايَةَ ثَنَائِكَ وَامَدَ مَجْدِكَ

وَانَّىٰ لِخَلِيقَتِكَ كُنْهُ مَعْرِفَةِ مَجْدِكَ

وَايَّ زَمَنٍ لَمْ تَكُنْ مَمْدُوحاً بِفَضْلِكَ

مَوْصُوفاً بِمَجْدِكَ

عَوَّاداً عَلَىٰ ٱلْمُذْنِبِينَ بِحِلْمِكَ

تَخَلَّفَ سُكَّانُ ارْضِكَ عَنْ طَاعَتِكَ

فَكُنْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ عَطُوفاً بِجُودِكَ

جَوَاداً بِفَضْلِكَ

عَوَّاداً بِكَرَمِكَ

يَا لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ انْتَ ٱلْمَنَّانُ

ذُو ٱلْجَلاَلِ وَٱلإِكْرَامِ
duas.transliteration:
allahumma inni aftatihu alqawla bihamdika

wa antiqu bilththana'i `alayka

wa umajjiduka wa la ghayata limadhika

wa uthni `alayka wa man yablughu ghayata thana'ika wa amada majdika

wa anna likhaliqatika kunhu ma`rifati majdika

wa ayya zamanin lam takun mamduhan bifadlika

mawsufan bimajdika

`awwadan `ala almudhnibina bihilmika

takhallafa sukkanu ardika `an ta`atika

fakunta `alayhim `atufan bijudika

jawadan bifadlika

`awwadan bikaramika

ya la ilaha illa anta almannanu

dhuljalali wal-ikrami
duas.translation:
O Allah, I commence my wording with declaring praise to You,

I utter the words of tribute to You,

I glorify You although Your glory is limitless,

and I compliment You although none can ever compliment You as much as You deserve and none can ever attain the extent of Your glory!

And how can Your creatures ever recognize Your glory [in its actual measure]!

And how can there be any moment for which You do not deserve praise due to Your favors

and in which You are not ascribed to glory

and in which You do not incessantly treat the sinful with Your forbearance?

Although the inhabitants of Your earth have fallen behind in being obedient to You,

You have been kind to them out of Your munificence,

munificent out of Your favors,

and liberal out of Your generosity.

O He save Whom there is no god! O All-benevolent!

O Lord of Glory and Honor!

segments.translation: O Allah, I commence my wording with declaring praise to You,
I utter the words of tribute to You,

segments.transliteration: wa umajjiduka wa la ghayata limadhika
segments.translation: I glorify You although Your glory is limitless,

segments.arabic: وَاثْنِي عَلَيْكَ وَمَنْ يَبْلُغُ غَايَةَ ثَنَائِكَ وَامَدَ مَجْدِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa uthni `alayka wa man yablughu ghayata thana'ika wa amada majdika
segments.translation: and I compliment You although none can ever compliment You as much as You deserve and none can ever attain the extent of Your glory!

segments.transliteration: wa anna likhaliqatika kunhu ma`rifati majdika
segments.translation: And how can Your creatures ever recognize Your glory [in its actual measure]!

segments.arabic: وَايَّ زَمَنٍ لَمْ تَكُنْ مَمْدُوحاً بِفَضْلِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa ayya zamanin lam takun mamduhan bifadlika
segments.translation: And how can there be any moment for which You do not deserve praise due to Your favors
and in which You are not ascribed to glory
and in which You do not incessantly treat the sinful with Your forbearance?
Although the inhabitants of Your earth have fallen behind in being obedient to You,
You have been kind to them out of Your munificence,
munificent out of Your favors,
and liberal out of Your generosity.

segments.arabic: يَا لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ انْتَ ٱلْمَنَّانُ
segments.transliteration: ya la ilaha illa anta almannanu
segments.translation: O He save Whom there is no god! O All-benevolent!
O Lord of Glory and Honor!

duas.title: 2nd Dua from Sahifa Radhvia
duas.description: It is incumbent upon you to remain attached with the Salaat of Ja’far-e-Tayyaar (a.s.) because it bears many benefits and merits. After finishing the salaat recite following supplication:
duas.arabic:
َللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْ اَسْاَلُكَ مِنْ كُلِّ مَا سَاَلَكَ بِهٖ مُـحَمَّدٌ وَ آلُهٗ وَ اَسْتَعِيْذُ بِكَ مِنْ كُلِّ مَا اسْتَعَاذَ مِنْهُ مُـحَمَّدٌ وَ آلُهٗ

َللّٰهُمَّ اَعْطِنِيْ مِنْ كُلِّ خَيْرٍ خَيْرًا وَ اصْرِفْ عَنِّيْ كُلَّ مَا قَضَيْتَ مِنْ شَرٍّ اَوْ فِتْنَةٍ وَ اغْفِرْ لِيْ مَا تَعْلَمُ مِنِّيْ وَ مَا قَدْ اَحْصَيْتَ عَلَيَّ مِنْ ذُنُوْبِيْ وَ اقْضِ حَوَآئِـجِيْ مَا لَكَ فِيْهِ رِضًا وَ لِيَ فِيْهِ صَلَاحٌ

ا يَا ذَا الْمَنِّ وَ الْفَضْلِ وَسِّعْ عَلَيَّ فِيْ الرِّزْقِ وَ الْاَجَلِ وَ اكْفِنِيْ مَا اَهَمَّنِيْ مِنْ اَمْرِ دُنْيَايَ وَ آخِرَتِيْ اِنَّكَ اَنْتَ عَلٰى كُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ قَدِيْرٌ.
duas.transliteration:
allaahumma innee as-aloka min kulle maa sa-alaka behi mohammadun wa aalohu wa as-ta-e’ezo beka min kulle mas ta-a’aza minho mohammadun wa aalohu.

allaahumma a-a’tenee min kulle khayrin khayran was rif a’nnee kulla maa qazayta min sharrin aw fitnatin wagh fir lee maa ta’lamo minnee wa maa qad ahsayta a’layya min zonoobee waq ze hawaaa-ejee maa laka feehe rezan wa leya feehe salaahun

yaa zal manne wal fazle wasse’ a’layya fir rizqe wal ajale wak fenee maa ahammanee min amre dunyaaya wa aakheratee innaka anta a’laa kulle shay-in qadeer.
duas.translation:
اO Allah! I beseech You from everything which Muhammad and his progeny have asked, and I take refuge with You from everything Muhammad and his progeny have sought refuge.

O Allah! Give me all best goodness, and keep away from me all what You have decreed from evil, or trial, and forgive me what You know about me, and that certainly You have recorded for me from my sins, and fulfill my needs in what You are pleased with and is in my interest,

O Lord of graciousness and favour! And expand for me sustenance and life-term, and save me from my grievances appertained to my world and my hereafter, surely You have power over everything.[1]

segments.arabic: َللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْ اَسْاَلُكَ مِنْ كُلِّ مَا سَاَلَكَ بِهٖ مُـحَمَّدٌ وَ آلُهٗ وَ اَسْتَعِيْذُ بِكَ مِنْ كُلِّ مَا اسْتَعَاذَ مِنْهُ مُـحَمَّدٌ وَ آلُهٗ
segments.transliteration: allaahumma innee as-aloka min kulle maa sa-alaka behi mohammadun wa aalohu wa as-ta-e’ezo beka min kulle mas ta-a’aza minho mohammadun wa aalohu.
segments.translation: اO Allah! I beseech You from everything which Muhammad and his progeny have asked, and I take refuge with You from everything Muhammad and his progeny have sought refuge.

segments.arabic: َللّٰهُمَّ اَعْطِنِيْ مِنْ كُلِّ خَيْرٍ خَيْرًا وَ اصْرِفْ عَنِّيْ كُلَّ مَا قَضَيْتَ مِنْ شَرٍّ اَوْ فِتْنَةٍ وَ اغْفِرْ لِيْ مَا تَعْلَمُ مِنِّيْ وَ مَا قَدْ اَحْصَيْتَ عَلَيَّ مِنْ ذُنُوْبِيْ وَ اقْضِ حَوَآئِـجِيْ مَا لَكَ فِيْهِ رِضًا وَ لِيَ فِيْهِ صَلَاحٌ
segments.transliteration: allaahumma a-a’tenee min kulle khayrin khayran was rif a’nnee kulla maa qazayta min sharrin aw fitnatin wagh fir lee maa ta’lamo minnee wa maa qad ahsayta a’layya min zonoobee waq ze hawaaa-ejee maa laka feehe rezan wa leya feehe salaahun
segments.translation: O Allah! Give me all best goodness, and keep away from me all what You have decreed from evil, or trial, and forgive me what You know about me, and that certainly You have recorded for me from my sins, and fulfill my needs in what You are pleased with and is in my interest,

segments.arabic: ا يَا ذَا الْمَنِّ وَ الْفَضْلِ وَسِّعْ عَلَيَّ فِيْ الرِّزْقِ وَ الْاَجَلِ وَ اكْفِنِيْ مَا اَهَمَّنِيْ مِنْ اَمْرِ دُنْيَايَ وَ آخِرَتِيْ اِنَّكَ اَنْتَ عَلٰى كُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ قَدِيْرٌ.
segments.transliteration: yaa zal manne wal fazle wasse’ a’layya fir rizqe wal ajale wak fenee maa ahammanee min amre dunyaaya wa aakheratee innaka anta a’laa kulle shay-in qadeer.
segments.translation: O Lord of graciousness and favour! And expand for me sustenance and life-term, and save me from my grievances appertained to my world and my hereafter, surely You have power over everything.[1]

duas.reference: Fiqh Imam Reza (a.s.), p. 155; Mustadrak al-Wasaael, vol. 6, pp. 224 & 231 (briefly); Behaar al-Anwaar, vol. 91, p. 209

duas.title: For Special needs - Severe Problems
duas.description:
One should perform Ghusl before the noon and if the problem is severe he should go in wilderness and pray Namaz-e-Ja’far-e-Tayyar. Shaykh al-Tusi has narrated that Imam al-Sadiq (`a) recommended the following prayer for having one’s requests granted:
Observe fasting on consecutive Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday. At Thursday evening, give half bushel of food for ten poor people as alms.
On Friday, wash yourself and go out to a wasteland and offer therein Ja`far al-Tayyar’s Prayer
Kneel down on the ground & You may then say the following prayer:
duas.arabic:
يَا مَنْ اظْهَرَ ٱلْجَمِيلَ

وَسَتَرَ ٱلْقَبِيحَ

يَا مَنْ لَمْ يُؤَاخِذْ بِٱلْجَرِيرَةِ

وَلَمْ يَهْتِكِ ٱلسِّتْرَ

يَا عَظِيمَ ٱلْعَفْوِ

يَا حَسَنَ ٱلتَّجَاوُزِ

يَا وَاسِعَ ٱلْمَغْفِرَةِ

يَا بَاسِطَ ٱلْيَدَيْنِ بِٱلرَّحْمَةِ

يَا صَاحِبَ كُلِّ نَجْوَىٰ

يَا مُنْتَهَىٰ كُلِّ شَكْوَىٰ

يَا مُقِيلَ ٱلْعَثَرَاتِ

يَا كَرِيـمَ ٱلصَّفْحِ

يَا عَظِيمَ ٱلْمَنِّ

يَا مُبْتَدِئاً بِٱلنِّعَمِ قَبْلَ ٱسْتِحْقَاقِهَا

يَا رَبَّاهُ يَا رَبَّاهُ يَا رَبَّاهُ
Repeat these 10 times each.
يَا اللَّهُ َيَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ
يَا سَيِّدَاهُ يَا سَيِّدَاهُ
يَا مَوْلاَيَاهُ يَا مَوْلاَيَاهُ
يَا رَجَاءَاهُ
يَا غِيَاثَاهُ
يَا غَايَةَ رَغْبَتَاهُ
يَا رَحْمٰنُ
يَا رَحِيمُ
يَا مُعْطِيَ ٱلْخَيْرَاتِ
صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
كَثِيراً طَيِّباً
كَافْضَلِ مَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَىٰ احَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ
After this mention your needs/desires.
duas.transliteration:
ya man azhara aljamila

wa satara alqabiha

ya man lam yu'akhidh biljarirati

wa lam yahtik alssitra

ya `azima al`afwi

ya hasana alttajawuzi

ya wasi`a almaghfirati

ya basita alyadayni bilrrahmati

ya sahiba kulli najwa

wa muntaha kulli shakwa

ya muqila al`atharati

ya karima alssafhi

ya `azima almanni

ya mubtadi'an bilnni`ami qabla istihqaqiha

yarabbahu ya rabbahu ya rabbahu
.
ya allahu ya allahu ya allahu
ya sayyidahu ya sayyidahu
ya mawlayahu ya mawlayahu
ya raja'ahu
ya ghiyathahu
ya ghayata raghbatahu
ya rahmanu
ya rahimu
ya mu`ti alkhayrati
salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin
kathiran tayyiban
ka-afdali ma sallayta `ala ahadin min khalqika
duas.translation:
O He Who has shown only goodness

and concealed the evil (deeds of His servants)!

O He Who has not censured for the offenses

and has not exposed the hidden!

O He Who is Ample in pardoning!

O He Who is Excellent in overlooking!

O He Who is Liberal in forgiveness!

O He Who opens His Hands wide with mercy!

O He Who is present in all confidential talks!

O He Who is the ultimate objective of all complaints!

O He Who raises from falls!

O He Who is Generous in condonation!

O He Who is Great in conferring bounties!

O He Who takes the initiative in favoring before they are deserved!

O my Lord, O my Lord, O my Lord!
.
O Allah, O Allah, O Allah!
O my Master, O my Master!
O my Chief, O my Chief!
O my hope!
O my succor!
O ultimate of my desire!
O All-beneficent!
O All-merciful!
O Giver of fortunes!
Bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
as much as and as pure as
You have ever blessed any of Your creatures.

segments.translation: O He Who has shown only goodness
and concealed the evil (deeds of His servants)!
O He Who has not censured for the offenses
and has not exposed the hidden!
O He Who is Ample in pardoning!
O He Who is Excellent in overlooking!
O He Who is Liberal in forgiveness!
O He Who opens His Hands wide with mercy!
O He Who is present in all confidential talks!
O He Who is the ultimate objective of all complaints!
O He Who raises from falls!
O He Who is Generous in condonation!
O He Who is Great in conferring bounties!
O He Who takes the initiative in favoring before they are deserved!

segments.arabic: يَا رَبَّاهُ يَا رَبَّاهُ يَا رَبَّاهُ
segments.translation: O my Lord, O my Lord, O my Lord!

segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya allahu ya allahu
segments.translation: O Allah, O Allah, O Allah!
O my Master, O my Master!
O my Chief, O my Chief!
O my hope!
O my succor!
O ultimate of my desire!
O All-beneficent!
O All-merciful!
O Giver of fortunes!
Bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
as much as and as pure as

segments.arabic: كَافْضَلِ مَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَىٰ احَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ
segments.transliteration: ka-afdali ma sallayta `ala ahadin min khalqika
segments.translation: You have ever blessed any of Your creatures.

duas.title: Dua from the book Rabi Al Anam
duas.description: After the last unit of the prayer of Ja’far Ibn Abi Taalib (a.s.), Imam Sadeq (a.s.) used to recite the following:
duas.arabic:
سُبْحَانَ اللهِ الْوَاحِدِ اْلأَحَدِ،

سُبْحَانَ اللهِ اْلأَحَدِ الصَّمَدِ،

سُبْحَانَ اللهِ الَّذِيْ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَ لَمْ يُولَدْ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ،

سُبْحَانَ اللهِ الَّذِيْ لَمْ يَتَّخِذْ صَاحِبَةً وَ لاَ وَلَدًا،

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لَبِسَ الْعِزَّ وَ الْوَقَارَ،

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ تَعَظَّمَ بِالْمَجْدِ وَ تَكَرَّمَ بِهِ،

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ أَحْصٰى كُلَّ شَيْ‏ءٍ عِلْمُهُ،

سُبْحَانَ ذِي الْفَضْلِ وَ الطَّوْلِ،

سُبْحَانَ ذِي الْمَنِّ وَ النِّعَمَ،

سُبْحَانَ ذِيْ الْقُدْرَةِ وَ اْلأَمْرِ،

سُبْحَانَ ذِيْ الْمُلْكِ وَ الْمَلَكُوتِ،

سُبْحَانَ ذِيْ الْعِزِّ وَ الْجَبَرُوْتِ،

سُبْحَانَ الْحَيِّ الَّذِيْ لاَ يَمُوتُ.

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ السَّمَاءُ بِأَكْنَافِهَا،

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ اْلأَرَضُوْنَ وَ مَنْ عَلَيْهَا،

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ الطَّيْرُ فِيْم أَوْكَارِهَا،

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ السِّبَاعُ فِيْم آجَامِهَا،

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ حِيْتَانُ الْبَحْرِ وَ هَوَا مُّهُ،

سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لاَلَيْنَبغِي التَّسْمِيح إِلَّا لَهُ،

سُبْحَنَمَنْ أَعصٰى كُلَّ شَيْ‏ءٍ عِلْمُهُ،

يَا ذَا النِّعْمَى وَ الطَّوَلِ يَا ذَا الْمَنِّ وَ الْفَوَلِّ،

يَا ذَا الْقوَى وَ الْكَرَمِ،

أَدَعُواكَ بِمَعَالَقَدِّ العزِّ مِن عَرشيكَ وَ مُنتهى الرَّحمة مِن كِتابكَ،

وَباسمكَ الأَعظَم الأَعلى وَ كَلِماتكَ التَّامَّات كُلّهَا،

أَنْ تُصَلِّي عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ أَنْ تَعْمَل بِي كذَا وَ كذَا.
duas.transliteration:
Subḥāna Allāhi al-wāḥidi al-aḥadi,

Subḥāna Allāhi al-aḥadi al-ṣamad,

Subḥāna Allāhi alladhī lam yalid wa lam yūlid wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan aḥad,

Subḥāna Allāhi alladhī lam yattaḵhiḏ ṣāḥibatan wa lā waladan,

Subḥāna man labisa al-‘izz wa al-waqār,

Subḥāna man ta‘aẓẓama bil-majd wa takarrama bihi,

Subḥāna man aḥṣā kulla shay'in ‘ilmuhu,

Subḥāna dhī al-faḍli wa al-ṭūl,

Subḥāna dhī al-manni wa al-ni‘am,

Subḥāna dhī al-qudrah wa al-amr,

Subḥāna dhī al-mulki wa al-malakūt,

Subḥāna dhī al-‘izz wa al-jabarūt,

Subḥāna al-ḥayy alladhī lā yamūt,

Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu al-samā'u bi-aknāfihā,

Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu al-arḍūn wa man ‘alayhā,

Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu al-ṭayru fī awkārihā,

Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu al-sibā‘u fī ājāmihā,

Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu ḥītān al-baḥri wa hawāmuhū,

Subḥāna man lā yanbaghī al-tasbīḥ illā lahu,

Subḥānaman aḥṣā kulla shay'in ‘ilmuhu,

Yā dhā al-ni‘mah wa al-ṭūl yā dhā al-man ni wa al-faḍl,

Yā dhā al-quwwati wa al-karam,

As'aluka bima‘qādi al-‘izz min ‘arshika wa muntahá ar-raḥmah min kitābika,

Wa bismika al-a‘ẓama al-a‘là wa kalimātika al-tammāt kulluhā,

An tuṣallī ‘alá Muḥammadin wa āli Muḥammadin wa an taf‘al bī kadhā wa kadhā.
duas.translation:
Glory be to Allah, the One, the Unique,

Glory be to Allah, the One, the Eternal,

Glory be to Allah, who neither begets nor is born, and there is none comparable to Him.

Glory be to Allah, who has taken neither a companion nor a child.

Glory be to Him who wears dignity and majesty.

Glory be to Him who is exalted in glory and honors it.

Glory be to Him who has knowledge of everything.

Glory be to the One of grace and abundance.

Glory be to the One of blessings and favors.

Glory be to the One of power and command.

Glory be to the One of sovereignty and dominion.

Glory be to the One of might and supremacy.

Glory be to the Ever-Living who does not die.

Glory be to Him whom the heavens glorify in their expanse.

Glory be to Him whom the earth and all that is upon it glorify.

Glory be to Him whom the birds glorify in their nests.

Glory be to Him whom the beasts glorify in their dens.

Glory be to Him whom the fish of the sea and its creatures glorify.

Glory be to Him for whom no glorification is due except for Him.

Glory be to Him who has knowledge of everything.

O You of grace and abundance! O You of blessings and favors!

O You of strength and generosity!

I ask You by the positions of honor from Your Throne and the ultimate mercy from Your Book,

And by Your greatest name and all Your perfect words,

That You send blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad and that You do for me thus and thus.

segments.translation: Glory be to Allah, the One, the Unique,
Glory be to Allah, the One, the Eternal,

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللهِ الَّذِيْ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَ لَمْ يُولَدْ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna Allāhi alladhī lam yalid wa lam yūlid wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan aḥad,
segments.translation: Glory be to Allah, who neither begets nor is born, and there is none comparable to Him.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللهِ الَّذِيْ لَمْ يَتَّخِذْ صَاحِبَةً وَ لاَ وَلَدًا،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna Allāhi alladhī lam yattaḵhiḏ ṣāḥibatan wa lā waladan,
segments.translation: Glory be to Allah, who has taken neither a companion nor a child.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لَبِسَ الْعِزَّ وَ الْوَقَارَ،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna man labisa al-‘izz wa al-waqār,
segments.translation: Glory be to Him who wears dignity and majesty.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ تَعَظَّمَ بِالْمَجْدِ وَ تَكَرَّمَ بِهِ،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna man ta‘aẓẓama bil-majd wa takarrama bihi,
segments.translation: Glory be to Him who is exalted in glory and honors it.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ أَحْصٰى كُلَّ شَيْ‏ءٍ عِلْمُهُ،
segments.translation: Glory be to Him who has knowledge of everything.
Glory be to the One of grace and abundance.
Glory be to the One of blessings and favors.
Glory be to the One of power and command.
Glory be to the One of sovereignty and dominion.
Glory be to the One of might and supremacy.
Glory be to the Ever-Living who does not die.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ السَّمَاءُ بِأَكْنَافِهَا،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu al-samā'u bi-aknāfihā,
segments.translation: Glory be to Him whom the heavens glorify in their expanse.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ اْلأَرَضُوْنَ وَ مَنْ عَلَيْهَا،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu al-arḍūn wa man ‘alayhā,
segments.translation: Glory be to Him whom the earth and all that is upon it glorify.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ الطَّيْرُ فِيْم أَوْكَارِهَا،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu al-ṭayru fī awkārihā,
segments.translation: Glory be to Him whom the birds glorify in their nests.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ السِّبَاعُ فِيْم آجَامِهَا،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu al-sibā‘u fī ājāmihā,
segments.translation: Glory be to Him whom the beasts glorify in their dens.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ حِيْتَانُ الْبَحْرِ وَ هَوَا مُّهُ،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna man sabbaḥat lahu ḥītān al-baḥri wa hawāmuhū,
segments.translation: Glory be to Him whom the fish of the sea and its creatures glorify.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لاَلَيْنَبغِي التَّسْمِيح إِلَّا لَهُ،
segments.transliteration: Subḥāna man lā yanbaghī al-tasbīḥ illā lahu,
segments.translation: Glory be to Him for whom no glorification is due except for Him.
Glory be to Him who has knowledge of everything.

segments.arabic: يَا ذَا النِّعْمَى وَ الطَّوَلِ يَا ذَا الْمَنِّ وَ الْفَوَلِّ،
segments.transliteration: Yā dhā al-ni‘mah wa al-ṭūl yā dhā al-man ni wa al-faḍl,
segments.translation: O You of grace and abundance! O You of blessings and favors!
O You of strength and generosity!

segments.arabic: أَدَعُواكَ بِمَعَالَقَدِّ العزِّ مِن عَرشيكَ وَ مُنتهى الرَّحمة مِن كِتابكَ،
segments.transliteration: As'aluka bima‘qādi al-‘izz min ‘arshika wa muntahá ar-raḥmah min kitābika,
segments.translation: I ask You by the positions of honor from Your Throne and the ultimate mercy from Your Book,

segments.arabic: وَباسمكَ الأَعظَم الأَعلى وَ كَلِماتكَ التَّامَّات كُلّهَا،
segments.transliteration: Wa bismika al-a‘ẓama al-a‘là wa kalimātika al-tammāt kulluhā,
segments.translation: And by Your greatest name and all Your perfect words,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّي عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ أَنْ تَعْمَل بِي كذَا وَ كذَا.
segments.transliteration: An tuṣallī ‘alá Muḥammadin wa āli Muḥammadin wa an taf‘al bī kadhā wa kadhā.
segments.translation: That You send blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad and that You do for me thus and thus.
